title: secale cereale var. arundinaceum
intro: Secale cereale var. arundinaceum, also known as wild rye or big alpine brome, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of wild rye include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural diuretic. Wild rye can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to address digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
